mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing the present value of S&P 500 equities and generating positive forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day returns occur in >60% of instances when the unemployment threshold is breached, or the signal loses predictive power during expansion phases.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and yielding positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive 60-day returns when either series diverges, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and manufacturing momentum, which translates into short-term positive equity returns as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during late-cycle phases, producing negative 5-day returns in >50% of cases when industrial production growth exceeds 2% quarter-over-quarter.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained consumer price index levels above 3.5% force expectations of aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Equity markets rally despite CPIAUCSL exceeding 3.5%, with 20-day forward returns positive in >65% of triggered months, invalidating the inflation-fear transmission channel.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a declining unemployment rate confirms a tightening labor market that supports wage growth and consumer spending, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal loses predictive accuracy when payroll growth exceeds 300k while unemployment remains flat, resulting in a negative Rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Higher real gross domestic product growth directly increases corporate profit margins and dividend capacity, establishing a persistent positive correlation with S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict 20-day forward returns, with the correlation coefficient remaining below 0.05 across all tested sub-periods and market conditions.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 1% signals immediate supply chain contraction and manufacturing weakness, triggering short-term risk aversion and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: The threshold breach consistently coincides with positive 5-day equity returns in >55% of cases, indicating that manufacturing contractions are priced as temporary noise rather than fundamental deterioration.
